title: What Scripture Actually Says About Waiting
caption:
You've been reading the waiting verse wrong.

"Wait on the Lord" — in Hebrew, that word is qavah. It means to twist together like strands of a cord. To bind yourself to Him. It's not a man on a couch. It's a man staying close to God while he does the work right in front of him.

The wilderness wasn't David's delay. It was his formation. He came out of it knowing what he was made of — something the palace never could have built in him.

And the desire you're carrying — for the wife, the role, the calling — bring it. Hold it open-handed. But a man who makes the desire the center of his prayers has started chasing the gift instead of the Giver.

Chase the man God's making you into. A man walking with God becomes someone worth following — and that's always been the point.

"Those who wait for the Lord shall renew their strength." — Isaiah 40:31.

Save this for the week it feels like nothing's moving.

#faith #scripture #christianmen #godlymen #manofgod #christianity #disciplineequalsfreedom #waiting
theme: He chases God, not a wife.
